zakatClaimEvidence: The Muslim Community Center Chicago explicitly mentions "Zakat requests" as one of its services. They have a "Rehab & Welfare/ Zakat Request" section on their website, detailing in-person and online application processes for zakat distribution, and provide an email address (zakat@mccchicago.org) for inquiries. Their Rehab and Welfare committee oversees "zakat requests and distributions" to assist families in need throughout the Chicagoland area.

all_citations.claim: The organization reported total revenue of $10,751,849, a 93.4% program expense ratio, and provides religious and educational services.
Financial profile metadata for Muslim Community Center Incorporate.
The organization maintains active community governance and operates programs including the MCC Academy.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization demonstrates strong financial health and high efficiency in its operations, though its overall score is moderated by a need for more public data regarding specific program outcomes.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing data transparency to improve overall impact and data confidence scores.
Developing more robust third-party verification for program-specific outcomes.

dimension_explanations.alignment: MCC shows high alignment for Muslim donors as a Zakat-eligible institution providing essential religious and cultural services [4]. The organization maintains a long-standing track record in the community, evidenced by its active governance and election processes [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through its full-time school and religious education programs, maintaining a high program expense ratio of 93.4% [1][3]. While financial health is robust with $10,751,849 in total revenue, the impact score reflects a medium level of data confidence regarding specific beneficiary outcomes [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim Community Center Incorporate serves as a cornerstone of Chicago's Muslim community through comprehensive religious services and educational excellence.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 93.4%, indicating the vast majority of funds directly support community services.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.03 per $1 raised.
Strong liquidity position with 23.6 months of working capital available.
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim Community Center Incorporate (MCC) is a multi-faceted religious and educational organization that provides daily congregations, marriage services, and funeral rites [1][3]. The organization operates the MCC Academy and offers extensive programs for the teaching and recitation of the Holy Quran for both children and adults [1][4].

alignment.improvement: Alignment could be further enhanced by <cite id="17">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> outside of the immediate Chicago metropolitan area.

credibility.explanation: MCC is a highly credible institution due to its <cite id="1">57-year history</cite> and <cite id="11">fully independent board of directors</cite>. Financial transparency is supported by <cite id="5">publicly available Form 990 filings</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization could <cite id="13">publish consistent beneficiary counts</cite> and seek external evaluations of its educational outcomes.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value long-term investment in established American Muslim community infrastructure and prioritize high financial efficiency in religious services.
donor_motivations: Want to support comprehensive Islamic K-12 education
Prefer organizations with extremely low fundraising overhead
Seek to fulfill zakat through a multi-service community hub
Value institutions with deep local roots and decades of history
giving_considerations: Donations support a large, established institution with significant assets
Impact is measured through service delivery rather than academic RCTs
Focus is primarily localized to the Chicago metropolitan area
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-growth international humanitarian relief or those who require rigorous third-party impact evaluations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that while activities are reported and financials are strong, there is no external evaluation of program outcomes.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: MCC creates impact by providing the physical and spiritual infrastructure necessary for a thriving Muslim community, though this pathway is not formally documented.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence helps donors understand the actual change created in students' lives and the community beyond just service volume.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the organization has strong reserves, its 17.8% growth rate suggests active expansion of services and facilities.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="6">93.4% of its expenses toward programs</cite> and maintains a remarkably low fundraising cost of <cite id="7">$0.03 per $1 raised</cite>.
MCC maintains <cite id="9">23.6 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term operational sustainability.
Operating since <cite id="1">1969</cite>, the organization has over five decades of experience in <cite id="4">religious and educational service delivery</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: The Muslim Community Center's financial health is characterized by high efficiency and robust growth. With a <cite id="10">3-year revenue CAGR of 17.8%</cite>, the organization has successfully scaled its reach while keeping overhead minimal. Its governance structure includes a <cite id="11">93.4% independent board</cite>, which oversees a diverse revenue stream where <cite id="12">$6,039,439 is generated through program services</cite>, such as school tuition and religious rites, reducing total reliance on volatile public donations.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Community Center Incorporate (MCC) serves as a foundational institution for the Chicago Muslim community, providing a comprehensive suite of religious, educational, and social services. Founded in <cite id="1">1969</cite>, the organization has grown into a significant community hub operating across <cite id="2">Chicago, Morton Grove, and Skokie</cite>. Its core operations include the <cite id="3">MCC Academy</cite>, which provides full-time K-12 education, alongside <cite id="4">daily and Friday congregations</cite>, funeral rites, and marriage services. Financially, the organization demonstrates substantial scale, reporting <cite id="5">$9,945,910 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. A defining characteristic of MCC is its operational efficiency; the organization maintains a <cite id="6">93.4% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that resources are directed toward its primary mission of community service and education. Furthermore, its fundraising model is exceptionally lean, costing only <cite id="7">$0.03 to raise every $1</cite> in donations. Beyond direct services, MCC acts as a philanthropic anchor, distributing <cite id="8">$714,141 in domestic grants</cite> to various recipients, including humanitarian organizations like MedGlobal and Helping Hand for Relief & Development.
